Which surface area should the candidate touch?
localized_focus.derived_focus_string: Add a new user-facing UX capability (CLI / slash command / output / docs / status rendering / dashboards) driven by a concrete recent pain. Refactors, optimizations, and infra-only work are out of scope.

completed_since_last.note: Shipped the full pipeline (planning → reviews → implement → cleanup → archive) in one day. Renderer skill 'coordinator-task-status-renderer' is now in the skill catalog. Unblocks kanban-viz's data-contract dependency.
Not on prior opportunity list; surfaced via git log.

items.title: Implement the freshly-planned kanban visualization SPA (no new proposal needed)
items.problem_statement: Operators reason about live multi-agent execution by tailing logs, reading tasks.md, and grepping .git-worktrees/.registry.json. Cognitive load grows superlinearly with parallelism — by the time three vendor-diverse reviewers are running across two work packages with overlapping file locks and a sync-point gate is silently blocked by a stale heartbeat, the operator has lost the plot. add-coordinator-kanban-viz proposes a 3-column Kanban (Backlog / In Flight / Done), vendor swimlanes on in-flight cards, and a sync-point gate banner — the three views that only make sense on this substrate.
items.hmw_reframe: How might we visualize multi-agent execution state without forcing operators to tail logs and grep registries?
items.localized_focus_alignment: Direct hit. New user-facing UX capability (a web SPA + native scaffold). Addresses chronic-multi-cycle pain (no global view, surfaced in April + multiple deferrals) AND fresh pain (planned within ~7 hours of this exploration; renderer dependency just landed). The proposal is already through Gate 1 with discovery decisions baked in.
items.why_now: Three signals align: (a) proposal is freshly planned with 71 tasks and Gate 1 approved; (b) its data-contract dependency (add-coordinator-task-status-renderer) shipped earlier today; (c) the alternative — replanning a smaller scope — would re-litigate decisions already made through review rounds. The MVP scope is deliberately narrow (3 columns + swimlanes + sync-point banner), with everything else (DAG view, time-slider, lock heatmap) explicitly deferred. Risk is medium not low because of (i) new Node toolchain in a Python repo and (ii) SSE auth handshake complexity, both bounded by scope.
items.parallel_feasibility: N/A — coordinator state not enumerated this run

items.title: Add /why-am-i-blocked <change-id> — one-command feature-stall explainer
items.problem_statement: Kanban-viz answers 'what is the state of all in-flight work?' on a board. It does NOT answer 'why is THIS specific change not advancing right now?' Today that requires fanning out across locks (check_locks), the work queue (list_issues), pending approvals (list_pending_approvals), missing OpenSpec artifacts (openspec list), worktree registry (worktree.py list), and the kanban-viz sync-point endpoint to assemble the answer. A single skill that takes a change-id and returns 'blocked by: <agent X holds lock on file Y since Tm ago | T2 awaiting approval N | tasks.md missing N1 → N2 dependency' closes a different gap from the board view: targeted diagnostic vs. ambient awareness.
items.hmw_reframe: How might we answer 'why is this feature stuck?' in one command instead of five sources?
items.localized_focus_alignment: New user-facing UX capability (slash command). Composes with kanban-viz (board surfaces 'something is blocked'; skill answers 'why' for that one card). Aligns with the attention-bottleneck framing the factory-missions work codified.
items.recommended_action: /plan-feature 'Add /why-am-i-blocked <change-id> skill that aggregates locks, work-queue state, pending approvals, missing artifacts, dependencies, and worktree heartbeat into a single answer to: why is this change not advancing right now?'
items.why_now: The numerical formula puts this candidate at 3.10 vs. kanban-viz at 3.025 — the effort discount (S vs M) pulls it ahead. Strategically the two are complementary, not competing: kanban-viz can be implemented while /why-am-i-blocked is being planned in parallel. The skill is small enough (~200 LOC: a CLI wrapper that calls existing coordinator endpoints and worktree.py and formats a structured answer) that it could ship in a single afternoon while kanban-viz implementation is underway.

items.title: Add /find-skill — fuzzy-search across the 80+ skill catalog with description previews
items.problem_statement: The skills directory now ships ~80 skills loaded into context at session start. Operator and new contributors cannot reasonably memorize the catalog; docs/skills-catalogue.md helps but is static and rots between updates. A short TUI (or even an interactive grep wrapper) that lets the operator type a fragment of intent ('triage prs', 'security check', 'plan a roadmap'), shows matching skills with their descriptions and trigger phrases, and offers to execute the chosen one closes the discoverability gap.
items.hmw_reframe: How might we surface the right skill at the right moment without making the operator memorize 80+ slash commands?
items.localized_focus_alignment: New user-facing UX capability addressing chronic discoverability pain. Orthogonal to kanban-viz and /why-am-i-blocked — different question, different surface. Not tied to a single recent commit, but the catalog has grown 30+% since skills-catalogue.md was last refreshed, so the pain is monotonically increasing.
items.recommended_action: Defer to third-tier — execute-add-coordinator-kanban-viz and add-why-am-i-blocked-skill are higher-leverage first moves.
items.why_now: Skill count crossed a memorability threshold this quarter. Static skills-catalogue.md is not refreshed by writes to skills/. A live tool stays current automatically.

items.title: Add /handoff-summary — structured pre-compact handoff renderer ('changes made / didn't touch / concerns')
items.problem_statement: Recent commits (2026-05-08 to 2026-05-12) show active iteration on auto-compact + pre-compact handoff in session-bootstrap. The handoff CONTENT is the cross-session UX surface — what the next session sees on resume. Today it's a coarse compact-summary; a structured renderer that emits 'changes made / didn't touch / concerns' (matching the PR description template the project already uses) gives the next session a daily-standup-quality starting point.
items.hmw_reframe: How might we make pre-compact handoffs as legible as a daily standup note?
items.localized_focus_alignment: New user-facing UX (cross-session continuity surface). Pain area was being actively worked 5/8–5/12; this proposal extends rather than competes with that work. Orthogonal to kanban-viz and /why-am-i-blocked.
items.recommended_action: Defer — promote only if compact-quality pain re-emerges after kanban-viz and /why-am-i-blocked ship.
items.why_now: Session-bootstrap is the active UX surface; layering a structured-output renderer on top is incremental and low-risk.

not_doing.alternative: Propose a smaller 'glance-page' instead of executing add-coordinator-kanban-viz
not_doing.rationale: Yesterday's regeneration proposed exactly this (`add-coordinator-glance-page`). It was a hand-waved 'minimal version' of the same idea. kanban-viz is the comprehensive version, already through Gate 1 with 71 tasks. Replanning would re-litigate decisions already made through review rounds and discard the value of having shipped the renderer specifically to be its data contract.

not_doing.alternative: Build any of the kanban-viz Out-of-Scope items now (DAG view, time slider, lock heatmap, cross-repo board, autopilot lane, WebSocket transport, multi-user auth, FalkorDB ingestion, write actions beyond drag-to-approve)
not_doing.rationale: Every one of these is explicitly deferred by the kanban-viz proposal's Out of Scope section, with documented reasons. Promoting one of them now competes with the MVP discipline and front-runs decisions the operator deliberately deferred.

not_doing.alternative: Native Swift menu-bar app (carried forward from April)
not_doing.rationale: Platform-locked to macOS, introduces a Swift/Xcode stack with zero repo reuse. The kanban-viz Tauri scaffold already provides a native-readiness path that works cross-platform and shares a codebase with the SPA.

not_doing.alternative: Standalone Grafana dashboards (carried forward from April)
not_doing.rationale: Orthogonal to the stated focus — Grafana is analytics/historical; the stated focus is 'real-time user-facing UX driven by recent pain.' Re-rank only if focus shifts to post-mortem analytics.

not_doing.alternative: Implement harness-engineering-features Feature 7 (Agent Throughput Dashboard) right now
not_doing.rationale: Overlaps Grafana. Report-only, not interactive. The 5-week stall at 0/21 suggests the parent proposal's scope is the obstacle. If Feature 7 specifically matters, carving it out as its own change would be the move — but kanban-viz already covers the real-time half of throughput visibility.

not_doing.alternative: Generate a fresh codeviz prototype variant via this skill
not_doing.rationale: codeviz is in active design iteration with cross-vendor feedback (ChatGPT Pro / Codex / Gemini). The right place to push that work is /prototype-feature on the existing roadmap, not /explore-feature. Inverting into 'just ship one variant' bypasses the deliberate convergence loop the operator set up there.

alternative_framing: The numerical formula puts add-why-am-i-blocked-skill at 3.10 (slightly above execute-add-coordinator-kanban-viz at 3.025) because S effort discounts more than M. If the operator has limited capacity this week, /plan-feature for /why-am-i-blocked is a faster win. If the operator can dedicate one session to implementation, execute kanban-viz first since the proposal is already through Gate 1 and the renderer dependency just landed. Best path if both are possible: implement kanban-viz on the main thread while /plan-feature /why-am-i-blocked runs in parallel.
